description: This figure presents a dual-axis plot showing the compositional dependence of yield stress and ductility in polycrystalline Fe–Al alloys (as-cast or annealed) as a function of aluminum content ranging from 0 to 40 at.%. The left y-axis displays yield stress (MPa) with data represented by solid triangles and a connecting solid line, while the right y-axis shows ductility (%) with data represented by filled circles and a dashed line. The yield stress curve exhibits a pronounced maximum of approximately 650 MPa at around 25% Al, attributed to solution hardening and short-range order hardening, followed by a sharp decline and subsequent gradual recovery at higher aluminum contents. Conversely, the ductility curve shows a general decreasing trend from approximately 35% at low aluminum contents to near 0% at 40% Al, but features a small yet distinct peak at approximately 28% Al. This anomalous ductility peak corresponds to a region where the work hardening rate reaches its maximum while the flow stress remains relatively constant, consistent with the Considère criterion for plastic instability. The data compilation includes measurements from the present study alongside literature data from Refs. [2,5,8], demonstrating reproducible mechanical property trends across different investigations.
